name: Zaid ibn Hasan
title: Witness to Karbala
shortDescription: The son of Imam Hasan Mujtaba (AS) who was by his uncle Imam Hussain's (AS) side in Karbala and was taken captive after Ashura.
fullStory:
Zaid ibn Hasan was the noble son of Imam Hasan Mujtaba (AS). He was a young man (in his twenties) at the time of the Karbala event and came to Karbala along with his uncle Imam Hussain (AS).

On the day of Ashura, Zaid ibn Hasan witnessed the brave battles and the martyrdom of his brothers (Qasim, Abdullah, and Abu Bakr) and his uncle. He was present during the battle, but after the martyrdom of Imam Hussain (AS) and the end of the fight, he was arrested by Umar Sa'd's soldiers along with the women and other survivors.

Zaid, who himself was mourning his brothers and uncle, traveled the harsh route of captivity from Kufa to Sham with chained hands and feet. His presence among the captives was another testament to the oppression of the Prophet's (PBUH) family.

After the captives' caravan returned to Medina, he adopted an ascetic life and until the end of his life (living over 90 years), kept the memory of Karbala's martyrs alive. He is considered one of the narrators of the events of Ashura and Kufa.
quote: (In description of the Bani Hashem captives:) Peace be upon those who were bound with chains and preserved God's religion with patience.
